prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. On January 4, 2023, Yariv Levin, Israel's recently appointed Justice Minister, declared his plan to implement several significant modifications to the judicial framework and the distribution of authority within Israel. Subsequently, on January 11, Levin released a preliminary version of his intended alterations, which encompassed substantial revisions to the operations and roles of the judiciary, executive, and legislative branches. The government's suggested reform has ignited considerable debate across Israel. Figures from the opposition, high-ranking former public officials, financial experts, campaigners, legal scholars, and various other groups have vehemently denounced the proposed changes, asserting that these changes will jeopardize judicial autonomy and essentially bestow unrestricted authority upon the government. Among those who object to the reform are reservists serving in Israel's security forces; some have indicated their refusal to perform reserve duty if the proposed reform advances to become law. They also encompass
B. On 4 January 2023, newly-appointed Israeli Justice Minister Yariv Levin announced his intention to make a number of major changes to the judicial system and the balance of powers in Israel. On 11 January, Levin published a draft of his proposed changes, which included significant changes to the judiciary, executive and legislative processes and functions. The government's proposed reform has sparked intense controversy in Israel. Opposition leaders, senior past civil servants, economists, activists, jurists and others have harshly criticized the proposed changes, arguing they will undermine judicial independence and effectively grant the government unchecked power. The opponents to the reform include reservists in Israel's security forces, with some stating they will stop conducting reserve duty service if the proposed reform is progressed through as legislation. They also include
C. On January 4, 2023, Yariv Levin, the newly appointed Israeli Justice Minister, announced his intention to make significant changes to the judicial system and balance of powers in Israel. He then released a draft of his proposed reform on 11 January, which included significant changes to the judiciary, executive, and legislative processes and functions. Israel has been deeply divided over the government's proposed reform, with opposition leaders, senior civil servants, economists, activists, jurists, and other critics, who claim they will grant the government unchecked, und
